column2:
Test instruction:
1. Before testing, please confirm whether the PC network card supports Gigabit. In addition, you need to confirm the network link speed and confirm it as follows: Control Panel--Network and Internet--View Network Status and Task--Change Adapter Settings-- Right click on "Local Network"--Properties--Configuration--Link Speed--Speed and Duplex Drop-down Menu
If you are testing a 100M NIC, set it to 100 Mbps full duplex. If you are testing a Gigabit NIC, set it to 1.0 Gbps full duplex.
2. Test Gigabit NICs with special support for Gigabit routers (ASUS RT-AC68U, TP-LINK WDR7500)
3, PC adb put iperf.exe tool, copy the iperf bin file to /system/bin, chmod 777
Test command:
TX: Platform input iperf -c [PC IP] -i 1 -t 60 -w 1m, PC input iperf -s
RX: input iperf -s on the platform side, iperf -c on the PC side [platform IP] -i 1 -t 60 -w 1m
4. Test the iperf data of TX/RX under each channel.
